title: arrhenatherum elatius var. brachycladum
intro: Arrhenatherum elatius var. brachycladum, also known as meadow oat-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, particularly artemisinin. The health benefits of meadow oat-grass include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural antiseptic. Meadow oat-grass can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, or it can be made into a tincture by extracting the plant's active compounds in a solvent.
